name: The First National Bank Of Coleraine
description_short: Community-focused bank established in 1906 offering personal and business banking, wealth management, and investment services with FDIC protection.
description_long:
The First National Bank of Coleraine was chartered on June 20, 1906, by John C. Greenway, a Teddy Roosevelt Rough Rider and innovative mining engineer. Originally established to serve residents and businesses in the mining industry, the bank has maintained its commitment to local community reinvestment for over 115 years. The bank was founded with a board that included Thomas F. Cole, Daniel M. Gunn, Chauncey A. McCarthy, Michael Curley, and John C. Greenway, whose leadership philosophy emphasized courage, common sense, loyalty, and service—principles the bank claims to carry forward today.

The First National Bank of Coleraine offers comprehensive banking services including personal banking, business banking, online and mobile banking, wealth management, and investment services through CETERA Investment Services. They provide financial calculators, home loan financing, and insurance products. The bank maintains FDIC insurance protection and Equal Housing Lender status. Online banking enrollment and a mobile banking app are available for account access and management.

The bank distinguishes itself primarily through its deep community history and stated commitment to knowing customers personally and reinvesting locally. Their 115-year heritage connected to Theodore Roosevelt and American frontier history provides distinctive branding. The emphasis on local relationship banking rather than large national operations sets them apart, along with their investment in community-focused services.

As a regional community bank, First National Bank of Coleraine is best suited for customers who value personal relationships, local reinvestment, and traditional banking services. However, the website provides limited details about specific product features, rates, fees, or competitive offerings. Potential customers should contact the bank directly to compare terms and features with larger national institutions.
services: Online banking platform with account access
pros: FDIC insured deposits providing federal protection up to $250,000
Over 115 years of continuous operation and community presence since 1906
Mobile banking app and online banking access for account management
Comprehensive services including personal banking, business banking, and wealth management
CETERA Investment Services partnership for investment portfolio management
Home loan programs available for mortgage financing
Stated commitment to local reinvestment and community support
cons: Website provides minimal information about specific products, rates, and fees—customers must call to get details
As a regional community bank, likely offers fewer digital features and less convenience than national or neobanks
Investment services are provided through third-party CETERA rather than in-house management
Limited transparency on website regarding account minimums, withdrawal limits, or specific account features
best_for: Community members and local business owners seeking relationship-based banking with personal service
Customers who prioritize local reinvestment and want deposits to support their community economy
Small business owners and entrepreneurs needing comprehensive business banking services
diagnosis: First National Bank of Coleraine is best for customers in their service area who value community banking, personal relationships, and local economic reinvestment over digital convenience or competitive rates. The main caveat is that the website lacks specific product details, rates, and fee information, requiring direct contact to evaluate whether their offerings are competitive with larger regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
